t: How much is a penny worth?
e: A penny is the smallest coin. It is worth 1¢.

t: How much is a nickel worth?
e: A nickel is worth 5¢. It is bigger than a penny but worth less than a dime.

t: How much is a dime worth?
e: A dime is worth 10¢. Even though it is small, it is worth more than a nickel!

t: How much is a quarter worth?
e: A quarter is worth 25¢. It is the largest common coin and worth the most.

t: Which coin is worth 5¢?
e: A nickel is the coin worth 5¢. It is larger than a dime but worth less.

t: Which coin is worth 10¢?
e: A dime is worth 10¢. It is the smallest silver-colored coin.

t: Which coin is worth the MOST?
e: A quarter is worth 25¢, which is more than a dime (10¢), nickel (5¢), or penny (1¢).

t: Which coin is worth the LEAST?
e: A penny is worth only 1¢. That is less than any other coin.

t: 2 nickels = ___ ¢
e: Each nickel is worth 5¢. Count by 5s: 5, 10. So 2 nickels = 10¢.

t: 3 pennies = ___ ¢
e: Each penny is worth 1¢. Count by 1s: 1, 2, 3. So 3 pennies = 3¢.

t: 2 dimes = ___ ¢
e: Each dime is worth 10¢. Count by 10s: 10, 20. So 2 dimes = 20¢.

t: 5 pennies = ___ ¢
e: Each penny is worth 1¢. Count 5 of them: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. So 5 pennies = 5¢.

t: True or false: A dime is worth more than a nickel.
e: True! A dime is worth 10¢ and a nickel is worth 5¢. 10¢ > 5¢.

t: Which coin is copper-colored and worth 1¢?
e: The penny is the copper-colored coin worth 1¢. All the other coins are silver-colored.

t: 3 dimes = ___ ¢
e: Each dime is worth 10¢. Count by 10s: 10, 20, 30. So 3 dimes = 30¢.

t: 4 nickels = ___ ¢
e: Each nickel is worth 5¢. Count by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20. So 4 nickels = 20¢.

t: How many cents is 1 quarter?
e: One quarter equals 25¢. Think of it as a quarter of a dollar (100¢ ÷ 4 = 25¢).

t: Put these coins in order from LEAST to MOST value: penny, quarter, dime, nickel.
e: From least to most: penny (1¢), nickel (5¢), dime (10¢), quarter (25¢).

t: 2 quarters = ___ ¢
e: Each quarter is worth 25¢. Count by 25s: 25, 50. So 2 quarters = 50¢.

t: 10 pennies have the same value as which coin?
e: 10 pennies = 10¢. A dime is also worth 10¢. So 10 pennies = 1 dime!
